Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Goal Fix an underfitting Procure to Pay process tree model Input Python like pseudocode of process tree Standard Logic 8 steps in order Create Req Approve Req Create PO Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case Current Issue PARALLEL block for steps 37 allows any order Output Requirements 1 Identify issues 2 Propose corrected tree 3 Explain fix

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Current Model Domain Logic Step1 Create Purchase Requisition Step2 Approve must follow Step1 Step3 Create PO must follow Step2 Step4 Receive Goods must follow Step3 Step5 Receive Invoice follows Step3 or4 before matching Step6 Match must follow3 4 5 Step7 Pay must follow6 Step8 Close must follow7 Current Model Flaw PARALLEL for Create PO Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Pay Allowed Bad Behavior Pay before Goods Match before Invoice Create PO after Goods Domain Constraint inherently sequential for core flow prompt standard logic implies strict linear sequence no branching

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Drafting the Solution Task1 Identify Issues PARALLEL implies no dependencies but PO before Goods Invoice Goods before Match Match before Pay Task2 Corrected Process Tree single SEQUENCE all 8 in order Task3 Explanation why Sequence enforces dependencies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Refinement In BPMN sometimes Goods and Invoice parallel converge at Match but prompt lists 45 sequential strict interpretation linear SEQUENCE Self Correction on Parallel nuance prompt standard logic numbered list implies 4 before5 linear most robust fix

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait one detail in prompt retain activities enforce sequence only normal order single flat sequence most restrictive but fixes underfitting Wait one more consideration possible Goods Invoice parallel but prompt defines standard logic as18 sequential deviate risks violating instruction sticking to linear
